result.instructions:
# php-xdebug-mcp
You are connected to a PHP debug adapter that bridges Xdebug DBGp into MCP.

## Preferred workflow
1. Call `xdebug_server_status` first to see if a session is already attached.
2. If none, call `xdebug_wait_for_session` (with a timeout) and trigger a debug request from the user side, OR ask the user to do so.
3. Call `xdebug_claim_session` to take exclusive control.
4. Set breakpoints with `xdebug_set_breakpoint` using local workspace paths (the adapter maps them to the runtime URI).
5. `xdebug_continue` until a break, then inspect: `xdebug_get_stack`, `xdebug_get_contexts`, `xdebug_get_variables`, `xdebug_get_property`, `xdebug_get_source`.
6. Step with `xdebug_step_into` / `xdebug_step_over` / `xdebug_step_out`. Use `xdebug_run_to_cursor` for a one-shot temporary line breakpoint.
7. When the investigation is finished, `xdebug_release_session` (and optionally `xdebug_stop` or `xdebug_detach` if allowed by config).

## Important behaviors
- This server does not expose a raw DBGp tunnel. Operations are typed and validated.
- Tools are gated by safety mode (current: `control`).
- `xdebug_break_execution` is only available if the engine reports `supports_async=1`.
- Continuation tools may take time. They return either a structured "still running" result or the new break/stop state. Keep your timeout reasonable.
- Synthetic frames (eval/internal) come back with `mapping_status=not_applicable`. Do not pass their `local_path` to other tools.
- All tool results follow the envelope `{ok, summary, data, warnings, session, next_actions}`.

## Resources
- `xdebug://sessions`, `xdebug://session/{id}`, `xdebug://session/{id}/stack`, `xdebug://session/{id}/breakpoints`, `xdebug://session/{id}/events`, `xdebug://session/{id}/stdout`, `xdebug://session/{id}/stderr`.
- `xdebug://session/{id}/source/{path}` for source retrieval (URL-encoded path).

## Path mapping
Workspace roots: /home/iclaborda/Pointerpro/survey-api
Configured rules:
- pointerpro: /home/iclaborda/Pointerpro/survey-api <-> /application

tools.name: xdebug_server_status
tools.description: Listener address, attached sessions, current safety mode and allow_stop/allow_detach flags.

tools.name: xdebug_list_sessions
tools.description: List all live and recently terminated debug sessions.

tools.name: xdebug_wait_for_session
tools.description: Block up to timeout_ms ms for an Xdebug session to attach (or for any session to reach expected_state).

tools.name: xdebug_get_session
tools.description: Snapshot of one session (auto-resolves to the only claimed session when session_id is omitted).

tools.name: xdebug_claim_session
tools.description: Take exclusive control of a session. Required before mutating tools.

tools.name: xdebug_release_session
tools.description: Release the claim on a session.

tools.name: xdebug_continue
tools.description: Continue execution until the next breakpoint or end of script.

tools.name: xdebug_step_into
tools.description: Step into the next statement; descend into function calls.

tools.name: xdebug_step_over
tools.description: Step over the next statement; do not descend into function calls.

tools.name: xdebug_step_out
tools.description: Run until the current function returns.

tools.name: xdebug_break_execution
tools.description: Interrupt a running session (requires supports_async).

tools.name: xdebug_wait_for_state
tools.description: Block up to timeout_ms until the session reaches expected_state.

tools.name: xdebug_stop
tools.description: Terminate the script via DBGp stop. The socket is expected to close.

tools.name: xdebug_detach
tools.description: Detach the debugger; the script continues without DBGp.

tools.name: xdebug_set_breakpoint
tools.description: Add a breakpoint. type=line|conditional|exception|call|return|watch. file_path is a local workspace path; the adapter maps it to the runtime URI.

tools.name: xdebug_list_breakpoints
tools.description: List adapter-registered breakpoints (and engine bindings if a session is given).

tools.name: xdebug_update_breakpoint
tools.description: Patch fields of an existing breakpoint. Patch keys: enabled, lineno, hit_value, hit_condition.

tools.name: xdebug_remove_breakpoint
tools.description: Remove a breakpoint and uninstall it from all live sessions.

tools.name: xdebug_run_to_cursor
tools.description: Install a temporary line breakpoint and prompt the agent to call xdebug_continue.

tools.name: xdebug_get_stack
tools.description: Read the current stack frames; each frame is normalised with mapping_status (file/eval/internal/unknown).

tools.name: xdebug_get_contexts
tools.description: Available variable contexts at a given stack depth.

tools.name: xdebug_get_variables
tools.description: List variables in a given context, with type and value (capped by max_data).

tools.name: xdebug_get_property
tools.description: Fetch a single property by fullname; supports paging.

tools.name: xdebug_get_source
tools.description: Retrieve source for a file at the runtime path; respects begin/end line range.

tools.name: xdebug_get_executable_lines
tools.description: Xdebug-specific: return the set of lines that may carry breakpoints in a given file.

tools.name: xdebug_get_typemap
tools.description: DBGp typemap for the current session.

tools.name: xdebug_configure_output
tools.description: Configure stdout/stderr capture (0=disable, 1=copy, 2=redirect).

tools.name: php_debug_run_cli

script.description: absolute path to a PHP script

tools.description: Spawn a PHP CLI script with XDEBUG_TRIGGER=1 pointing at this adapter.

tools.name: php_debug_http_request
tools.description: Fire an HTTP request with the XDEBUG_SESSION cookie to start a debug session.
